title: Adani Power Receives Letter of Award for 2500 MW Power Supply Contract from MSEDCL - scanx.trade
content:
Adani Power Receives Letter of Award for 2500 MW Power Supply Contract from MSEDCL
Adani Power Limited has been awarded a significant 25-year contract by Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited for supplying 2500 MW renewable energy round-the-clock power. The Letter of Award was received following a successful e-reverse auction process, with the contract providing long-term revenue stability while supporting Maharashtra's renewable energy objectives and India's clean energy goals.
*this image is generated using AI for illustrative purposes only.
Adani Power Limited has secured a major renewable energy contract from Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL) for the supply of 2500 MW renewable energy round-the-clock (RE RTC) power. The company received the Letter of Award on April 1, 2026, at 8:30 PM, after being declared the successful bidder in MSEDCL's e-reverse auction process.
Contract Details and Specifications
The power supply agreement encompasses several key parameters that highlight the scale and duration of this renewable energy commitment:
Regulatory Compliance and Documentation
Adani Power fulfilled its regulatory obligations by notifying both major stock exchanges about this significant development. The company submitted the required intimation to BSE Limited and National Stock Exchange of India Limited under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosures Requirements) Regulations, 2015.
The formal documentation process was completed with reference to SEBI Master Circular bearing reference number HO/49/14/14(7)2025-CFD-POD2/I/3762/2026 dated January 30, 2026. Company Secretary Puneet Bansal signed the regulatory filing on April 2, 2026.
Strategic Implications
This contract award represents a substantial commitment to renewable energy infrastructure development in Maharashtra. The 25-year duration provides long-term revenue visibility for Adani Power while supporting the state's renewable energy objectives.
Key aspects of the agreement include:
- Long-term stability: 25-year contract duration ensures sustained revenue generation
- Renewable focus: RE RTC power supply aligns with India's clean energy goals
- Scale advantage: 2500 MW capacity represents significant market presence
- Domestic opportunity: Contract awarded by domestic entity supports local energy security
The company confirmed that this contract does not involve any related party transactions and that promoter groups have no interest in the awarding entity, ensuring arm's length commercial terms.
